## Shortcode System

The bot uses a shortcode system for Discord emojis, stickers, and GIFs. Agent writes shortcodes in messages and the parser expands them before sending to Discord.

### Syntax

- **Emoji:** `:Name:` → inline, expands to `<a:Name:ID>` (or unicode fallback if not registered)
- **Sticker:** `:sticker:Name:` → extracted from message, sent as a separate Discord sticker
- **GIF:** `:gif:Name:` → extracted from message, sent as a separate message with the GIF URL

### Adding Shortcodes

Use `/add-shortcode` to register custom Discord emojis, stickers, or GIFs. This uploads the asset to your server and adds it to `src/shortcodes.ts`.

If no custom emojis are registered, the bot falls back to unicode emojis automatically via `resolveEmoji()`.

### Shortcode Table

<!-- Add your shortcodes here as you register them with /add-shortcode -->
<!-- Format: | :Name: | tags | example usage | -->

No shortcodes registered yet. Use `/add-shortcode` to add your first one.

## Vault

Obsidian vault in `vault/` directory. Long-term memory, people, projects, notes, and skills.

```
vault/
  memory/
    MEMORY.md      — Long-term curated memory
    YYYY-MM-DD.md  — Daily logs
  people/          — People profiles and relationships
  projects/        — Project notes and decisions
  notes/           — Learnings, ideas, video notes
  downloads/       — Downloaded files (gitignored)
```

Use wiki-links (`[[Name]]`) so Obsidian's graph view shows connections between entries.
